title: Candidate-Elimination Algorithm Version Space Reduction
description: A diagram illustrating how the version space (the set of all consistent hypotheses) is iteratively reduced by the most general (G) and most specific (S) boundary sets as they are updated with positive and negative training examples.
relationships: A positive example forces S to become more general
A negative example forces G to become more specific
G and S converge towards the target concept
The version space is the set of hypotheses between S and G
